Demurrer To Pltf'S Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-08-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Peggy Chang, Esq. (510-548-7474) of Buresh, Kaplan, Feller & Chang for Plaintiff/ opposing party Ethan Chan.
Justin McNeill, Esq. (415-434-3490) for Defendant/Moving party Howard Lewis.

The Court announces the tentative ruling. Defendant submits to the tentative ruling. ARGUED. Plaintiff argues the 6th Cause of action only, Newhall, seller has an obligation/duty to disclose to the buyer. Defendant argues exhibit G. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling except for the 6th cause of action as follows:

Demurrer To Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint is Sustained in part and overruled in part. The 6th cause of action is taken under submission.

The demurrer is overruled as to the first cause of action alleged against Mr. Lewis as trustee of the Jennie V. Maybon Trust. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh, and eighth causes of action alleged against Mr. Lewis both individually and in his capacity as trustee. Apart from the court's October 18, 2016 order on Mr. Lewis' demurrer to the first amended complaint and the propriety of the Chans' amendment adding three additional causes of action, the Chans' opposition to this motion is treated as a request for leave to amend.

As to the first cause of action, the Chans adequately allege a claim for negligent misrepresentation against Mr. Lewis in his capacity as trustee of the Maybon Trust. The complaint indicates Defendant RE/MAX was acting as an agent of Mr. Lewis as trustee when advertising the property. Any alleged misrepresentations are attributable to Mr. Lewis as principal of its agent RE/MAX. As to the first cause of action alleged against Mr. Lewis individually, there are no facts alleging Mr. Lewis made misrepresentations in his individual capacity and it is not reasonably possible this defect can be cured by amendment.

The sixth cause of action is taken under submission whether it is sustained with or without leave to amend. The sixth cause of action does not allege a cognizable negligent nondisclosure claim because Mr. Lewis was under no duty to disclose facts within the reach of the diligent attention and observation of the buyer. The court in Newhall Land and Farming Co. v. Superior Court (1993) 19 Cal.App.4th 334, 349, stated "where the seller knows of facts materially affecting the value or desirability of the property'and also knows that such facts are not known to, or within the reach of the diligent attention and observation of the buyer, the seller is under a duty to disclose them to the buyer."

Assuming without deciding Labor Code 2750.5 is inapplicable here, the seventh cause of action fails to allege a cognizable negligence claim because property sold "as-is" relieves the seller of all liability for defective conditions other than liability based on fraud, misrepresentation, or nondisclosure. (Shapiro v. Hu (1986) 188 Cal.App.3d 324, 332.)
The eighth cause of action fails to allege a cognizable negligence per se claim and the Chans do not oppose the demurrer as to this cause of action.

Defendant submits a proposed order. Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-18-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. EVAN CHAN OF THE CHAN LEGAL GROUP, TEL. NO. 1-415-624-3014 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. RICHARD J. USS OF THE CARLSON LAW GROUP, INC., TEL. NO. 1-818-996-7800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS BY TELEPHONE APPEARANCE.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Howard Lewis' demurrer to the first amended complaint is: a) sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for breach of contract and the third cause of action for breach of the implied covenant alleged against Mr. Lewis in his individual capacity as distinct from his being a trustee; b) sustained with twenty days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for negligence alleged against Mr. Lewis both individually and as trustee and the second cause of action for breach of contract alleged against Mr. Lewis as trustee; and c) overruled as to the third cause of action for breach of the implied covenant alleged against Mr. Lewis as trustee and the fourth cause of action alleged against Mr. Lewis both individually and as trustee. Because the first amended complaint and the judicially noticeable grant deed show that the Trust owned the property and that Mr. Lewis in his individual capacity was not a party to the sales agreement, there is no basis for a contract claim against Mr. Lewis in his individual capacity. The first cause of action does not allege a cognizable negligence claim. Plaintiffs Ethan Chan and Regina Chan are given leave to allege a claim for negligent misrepresentation. The breach of contract claim fails against Mr. Lewis as trustee because the Chans have not alleged that the Trust breached an express term of the contract. Mr. Lewis' argument that additional portions of the contract need to be alleged or attached to the first amended complaint lacks merit. The purchase contract is attached and a party can plead a contract according to its legal effect. The implied covenant claim sufficiently alleges conduct showing that the Trust interfered with the right of the Chans to receive the benefits of the contract. Paragraphs 11-12, 14-15, and 63-71 of the first amended complaint allege a fraud claim with the requisite particularity. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 =302/hk
